source.multipart_basis: FSG TSAC enclosures are fabricated as bolted/welded multi-panel aluminum boxes. Decomposition reflects canonical accumulator container practice: structural base plate carrying cell module pickups, four side walls, an HV interconnect feedthrough plate, busbar mounting rails that internally carry cell-module reaction into the walls, cable gland plates for the HV/LV harness exits, a ventilation grate cover, a removable top cover, and the eight chassis mounting feet. Speculative at the level of exact panel layout; load magnitudes, material, and pass/fail thresholds are preserved from the single-body brief.

full_prompt:
Design the Tractive System Accumulator Container (TSAC) enclosure for a student-built electric open-wheel race vehicle. Accumulator total mass (cells + BMS + hardware inside the container) = 80 kg; tractive system DC voltage nominal 400 V (must be <= 600 V DC per governing structural-demonstration rules). Container is a single monolithic TIG-welded aluminum alloy 5083-H116 box (E = 72 GPa, yield = 215 MPa, ultimate = 305 MPa, density = 2660 kg/m^3). Overall outer bounding box 650 mm (X, forward) x 450 mm (Y) x 400 mm (Z); wall thickness to be sized (typical 3 mm for side walls, 4 mm for base). Mounted to chassis at 8 locations using M10 Grade 8.8 bolts on a pattern that distributes load paths. Requirements: (R1) Forward inertial loading: 40 g forward applied to the full 80 kg battery mass = 40 x 80 x 9.81 = 31,400 N distributed through the cell module mounting points into the container structure; max von Mises in container walls and welds <= yield / 1.1 = 195 MPa (weld-zone factor). (R2) Lateral (side) inertial loading: 20 g x 80 kg x 9.81 = 15,700 N; max von Mises <= 195 MPa. (R3) Vertical (up and down) inertial loading: 20 g x 80 kg x 9.81 = 15,700 N; max von Mises <= 195 MPa. (R4) No single container fastener, anchor, or weld may fail under the ultimate load of the worst-case case (1.0 x its case load, since the 40 g / 20 g numbers are already ultimate); fastener shear + tension check via VDI 2230 proxy; utilization < 1.0. (R5) Container mass (excluding cells) <= 15 kg. (R6) The container must electrically isolate the tractive system; no structural bolt penetrates the insulating liner. (R7) First modal frequency hard-mounted at the 8 bolt holes >= 40 Hz (avoid 2x chassis mode ~25 Hz). Primary verification: linear static FEA for R1-R3, bolt check for R4, modal for R7. Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation NOT required (thermal runaway is a separate safety gate). Deliverables: STEP AP242 welded container (treated as a single FEA body), weld map, and a report listing container mass in kg, max von Mises per load case in MPa, bolt utilization, first modal frequency in Hz.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
This design is delivered as a multi-part welded/bolted aluminum assembly rather than a single monolithic shell. Speculated part families for a TSAC container: (A) structural base plate carrying eight chassis mounting feet and the cell-module pickup pattern; (B) four side walls (long-forward, long-rear, left, right); (C) removable top cover bolted into a perimeter seal rail; (D) HV interconnect feedthrough plate (high-voltage connector bulkhead on the rear wall, electrically isolated); (E) internal busbar mounting rails that carry cell-module reaction into the long walls; (F) cable gland plates on the LV/comms side; (G) ventilation grate / pressure-relief cover satisfying the rulebook venting path; (H) chassis mounting feet (x8) with M10 Grade 8.8 bolt bosses. Interfaces: base<->walls (TIG fillet weld); walls<->walls corner seam (TIG fillet); top cover<->walls (bolted with EPDM gasket, electrically isolated); feedthrough plate<->rear wall (bolted with insulating O-ring); busbar rails<->walls (bolted threaded inserts); gland plate<->side wall (bolted); vent cover<->top cover (bolted); feet<->base (TIG fillet or bolted-through with internal backing plate). FEM verification runs on the assembled model ONLY per fem_check_policy=assembled_only; per-part von Mises is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection DCR <= 1.0 under the worst of LC1/LC2/LC3. Load magnitudes, material properties, mass cap (15 kg total container), and pass/fail thresholds are preserved verbatim from the monolithic brief.
short_prompt:
Design the Tractive System Accumulator Container (TSAC) enclosure for a student-built electric open-wheel race vehicle as a multi-part welded + bolted aluminum assembly. Accumulator total mass (cells + BMS + hardware inside) = 80 kg; tractive system DC voltage nominal 400 V (<= 600 V DC per governing structural-demonstration rules).

Material: Al 5083-H116 welded (E = 72 GPa, yield = 215 MPa, ultimate = 305 MPa, density 2660 kg/m^3). Outer bounding box 650 mm (X forward) x 450 mm (Y) x 400 mm (Z); typical wall 3 mm side, 4 mm base. Mounted to chassis at 8 locations using M10 Grade 8.8 bolts.

LC1 forward inertial 40 g on 80 kg = 31,400 N distributed through cell module mounting points; max von Mises in walls and welds <= yield / 1.1 = 195 MPa (weld-zone factor). LC2 lateral 20 g x 80 kg = 15,700 N; max von Mises <= 195 MPa. LC3 vertical 20 g x 80 kg = 15,700 N; max von Mises <= 195 MPa. Bolt utilization via VDI 2230 proxy < 1.0. Container mass (excluding cells) <= 15 kg. Electrical isolation: no structural bolt penetrates the insulating liner. First modal frequency hard-mounted at 8 bolt holes >= 40 Hz.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Fastener utilization under combined shear + tension is a VDI 2230 calculation done outside FEA.

TIG fillet welds at base-to-wall and wall-to-wall seams are sized by AWS D1.2 / FSAE accumulator-rule weld-zone factor (yield/1.1). Weld throat utilization is computed from FEA line-reactions, not from continuum stress.

prompt.title: Student EV tractive-system battery enclosure (multi-part welded/bolted assembly)
prompt.brief: 5083-H116 welded+bolted accumulator container for 80 kg tractive system; survives 40 g forward / 20 g side / 20 g vertical inertial loads. Multi-part assembly: base plate (A), four side walls (B1-B4), removable top cover (C), HV feedthrough plate (D), internal busbar mounting rails (E), cable gland plates (F), ventilation/pressure-relief cover (G), chassis mounting feet (H x8). FEM at assembled level only; connection DCR <= 1.0 under governing LC.

geometric_constraints.outer_bounding_box_mm: 650 x 450 x 400 (X forward x Y x Z)

mount_bolts.pattern_note: distributed so that LC1 40 g forward load shares among no fewer than 4 of the 8 feet

geometric_constraints.electrical_isolation: no structural bolt penetrates the internal insulating liner (R6); HV feedthrough uses an insulating O-ring and isolated ground path

material.name: Al 5083-H116 welded
material.gasket_material: EPDM or equivalent closed-cell, non-structural (contributes no stiffness in FEA)

load_cases.name: forward_40g
load_cases.distribution: coupled to interior cell-module mounting pickups on the base plate (A) and busbar rails (E)

load_cases.name: lateral_20g
load_cases.distribution: coupled to interior cell-module pickups; reacted primarily by long walls B1/B2

load_cases.name: vertical_20g
load_cases.distribution: coupled to interior cell-module pickups; reacted primarily by base A and top cover C with gasket non-structural

load_cases.name: modal
load_cases.description: hard-mounted at 8 foot bolt holes, first modal frequency target >= 40 Hz

boundary_conditions.mounting: 8 foot bolt holes on part H (chassis mounting feet) fixed in all 6 DOF (chassis is rigid relative to container for this check)
boundary_conditions.internal_mass: 80 kg battery pack lumped and RBE3-coupled to cell-mount interior surfaces on A and E
boundary_conditions.gasket_handling: gasket E between C and B1-B4 modeled as a contact pair with zero tensile capacity (compression only); no stiffness contribution

objective.design_heuristic: target total mass <= 15 kg excluding cells

output_format.body_count: multi-body (A + B1-B4 + C + D + E-rails + F-gland plates + G + H x8)

parts.name: base_plate
parts.role: structural floor carrying the cell-module mounting pattern and transferring all inertial loads from the battery into the 8 chassis mounting feet

geometric_constraints.interface_to_feet: welded fillet or through-bolted with internal backing

parts.name: side_wall_panel
parts.role: vertical sidewall; four instances (forward, rear, left, right) forming the enclosure box and carrying in-plane shear under LC1 and LC2

geometric_constraints.corner_joint: TIG fillet weld at vertical seams between adjacent walls
geometric_constraints.top_edge_feature: gasket rail with threaded inserts for top-cover bolts

parts.name: top_cover
parts.role: removable lid providing service access and closing the pressure-relief / venting path

geometric_constraints.sealing: EPDM gasket seated on B-family top rail

parts.name: hv_interconnect_feedthrough_plate
parts.role: bulkhead penetration carrying the HV connector and maintaining electrical isolation to R6

geometric_constraints.isolation: insulating O-ring + non-conductive sleeve around all bolt penetrations

parts.name: busbar_mounting_rail
parts.role: internal longitudinal rails that carry the cell-module reaction loads into the long walls and keep busbars clear of the wall interior surfaces

geometric_constraints.cross_section_mm: 25 x 25 x 3 hollow or solid rectangular
geometric_constraints.attachment: bolted to B-left and B-right via threaded inserts; no weld (to preserve insulating liner)

parts.name: cable_gland_plate
parts.role: LV/comms harness exits; bolted plate with IP-rated gland fittings

parts.name: ventilation_grate_cover
parts.role: pressure-relief / vent path cover on the top cover; passes the FSAE-rule venting requirement without forming an electrical path

parts.name: chassis_mounting_foot
parts.role: load-path foot welded or through-bolted to the base plate; carries the M10 8.8 chassis bolt and distributes container inertial reactions into the chassis node

geometric_constraints.distribution: two per corner area, placed so that no fewer than 4 feet carry load simultaneously under LC1

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: X forward (vehicle), Y left-right, Z vertical
assembly_envelope.interior_clear_volume_note: must accommodate the 80 kg pack, BMS, and busbars without wall interference

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all inter-part interfaces (IF1..IF8) <= 1.0 under the governing load case (worst of LC1/LC2/LC3).
Weld-seam throat utilization at base-to-wall and wall-to-wall TIG fillets <= 1.0 using weld-zone allowable 195 MPa.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: shell or solid-shell for plates; beam or solid for busbar rails and feet
connection_modeling: TIG fillet welds as tied contact with throat-size capacity check post-process
top-cover bolts and feedthrough bolts as stiffness-equivalent CBUSH or MPC pairs

interfaces.mediated_by: TIG fillet weld, continuous around base perimeter
interfaces.load_path: battery inertial reaction into base then into walls; primary LC1/LC2 shear path

interfaces.mediated_by: TIG fillet weld at vertical corner seams
interfaces.load_path: corner shear tie; prevents wall opening under LC1 forward load

interfaces.mediated_by: perimeter bolts (>=12) with EPDM gasket, compression-only contact
interfaces.load_path: LC3 +Z uplift into top cover, back through bolts to walls

interfaces.mediated_by: bolted with insulating O-ring and non-conductive sleeves
interfaces.load_path: HV connector reaction into rear wall; preserves electrical isolation R6

interfaces.mediated_by: bolted threaded inserts (no weld to preserve insulating liner)
interfaces.load_path: cell-module reaction into long walls; primary LC2 lateral path
harness strain reaction (minor) into side wall

interfaces.mediated_by: TIG fillet weld or through-bolt with internal backing plate
interfaces.load_path: primary reaction path from container to chassis; governs LC1 forward and bolt check R4

fem_check_policy.rationale: TSAC containers are multi-panel welded+bolted assemblies; the governing stress states arise at the weld toes and bolted interfaces between panels. Single-panel FEA would mis-estimate load sharing between base, walls, cover, and feet. Per-part stress is reported for diagnostics but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: Real FSAE/FSG accumulator containers are TIG-welded aluminum boxes closed by a bolted top cover with insulated HV feedthroughs; the 'monolithic' framing in the original brief is a modeling simplification, not a manufacturing reality.
notes.speculation_caveats: Exact wall partition, busbar-rail count, gland-plate placement, and foot geometry are team-specific. Counts and dimensions here are plausible defaults for a 650x450x400 mm container; no FSG rulebook clauses are cited beyond what the single-body brief already carries.

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Thermal runaway and venting are covered under a separate safety gate.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Box walls in bending; buckling not driving mode.
